summary: Mixed‑quality recording with warbly filler tracks and some high‑grade SBD sections, while the show offers energetic moments (Good Lovin, Dark Star) but suffers from vocal lapses.
review: The release combines a rough audience‑style filler that sounds “beat up” with a cleaner soundboard source for the Dark Star segment, resulting in an overall fair listening experience. The performance has flashes of excitement—especially the crowd‑driven Good Lovin and a sweet China>Rider>Estimated>Eyes sequence—but is marred by vocal slip‑ups and uneven musical focus. Track‑gap editing issues also detract from flow.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed (good moments but vocal/musical lapses) and recording quality varies between poor audience‑style filler and decent soundboard sections, leading to a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First filler (drums/Good Lovin) is warbly and sounds like an old audience tape; Dark Star filler is a clean SBD source. Reviewers note long dead‑silence gaps before songs and overall inconsistent editing.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The main set contains energetic peaks (notably Good Lovin and the China‑Rider‑Estimated‑Eyes run) but is interrupted by vocal lapses and uneven pacing, further hampered by long silence gaps between tracks.
